name: Engineering Finding Schema + Fixtures (PR-F2, handover F-009)

design_notes.nature_of_this_feature: Handover F-009. Add schema/engineering_finding.schema.json: the machine-readable JSON Schema for a single engineering finding, whose PROSE convention feature 035 already shipped in ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md Sec 7 ('The findings artifact's shape (severity, confidence per Section 5, evidence per Section 3, impact, owner, and closure criteria) is defined by schema/engineering_finding.schema.json. This protocol governs the artifact's required semantics; it does not itself define the JSON Schema (added by a later feature in this phase)'). The protocol document is the semantic authority; this feature only encodes its declared shape as a validatable schema, plus positive and negative fixtures, plus indexing the new schema in NIZAM.json and schema/README.md -- directly mirroring feature 038's shipped, fully-amended design (schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json, .agent/contracts/038.json rev 3) and feature 037's proposed design (schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json, .agent/contracts/037.json) as exemplars. Per .agent/feature_list_005.json feature 039 acceptance core: (1) 'Severity, confidence, evidence, impact, owner, closure criteria supported' -- all six are required top-level keys; (2) 'Positive and negative fixtures exist' -- realized as TWO positive fixtures (one open, one resolved-with-complete-closure-evidence, proving the conditional's happy path in both states) and THREE negative fixtures matching the handover's own minimum case ('a finding marked resolved without closure evidence is rejected') plus two further probes closing the exact content-completeness gap class .agent/qa/038-contract-review.json's HIGH finding identified (a closure_evidence key present but structurally empty; a required field -- owner -- missing entirely). This feature does NOT author or amend ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md's prose (035's scope, already shipped), does NOT define the CLI that produces findings.json (041's scope), does NOT define the preflight-verdict or baseline schemas (038's already-shipped schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json; 037's schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json), and does NOT encode ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md's own new/resolved/reopened/stale classification of finding SETS across two compared executions (036's scope; that protocol classifies comparisons between two audits, not a single finding's own required fields -- see design_notes.severity_and_status_scope below).
design_notes.severity_and_status_scope: TWO deliberate, protocol-anchored design decisions the reviews should specifically re-verify: (1) 'severity' is required and non-empty (minLength 1) but is DELIBERATELY NOT a fixed value enum. ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md Sec 1 states verbatim: 'Consumers extend this protocol with their own repository- and ecosystem-specific finding categories and severities; they do not redefine its evidence hierarchy, its maturity model, its finding confidence vocabulary, or its commercial-readiness exclusion' -- severity is explicitly named as a consumer extension point, NOT one of the four protocol-fixed mechanics. Hardcoding a specific severity enum here (e.g. ['P0','P1','P2','P3'] or ['critical','high','medium','low']) would redefine something the audit protocol's own Sec 1 reserves for consumers, and is therefore a fidelity defect this contract deliberately avoids -- not an oversight. 'confidence', by contrast, IS one of the protocol's four fixed mechanics (Sec 5) and is therefore a closed three-value enum. (2) 'status' (open/resolved) is a WITHIN-FINDING lifecycle flag gating the closure_evidence conditional, and is NOT the same vocabulary as ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md Sec 3's four finding-state TRANSITION classes (new/resolved/reopened/stale), which classify a finding's movement ACROSS two compared executions (036's scope, a comparison-level classification of finding SETS). The two constructs share the word 'resolved' because both describe a finding that has been closed with evidence, but this schema's 'status' enum intentionally has only two values (open/resolved) because no acceptance test in this feature requires representing 'reopened' or 'stale' at the single-finding-schema level -- those are properties of a COMPARISON's output, not of an individual finding record.
design_notes.schema_design: Top-level object, eight required keys: 'id' (string, minLength 1, unique within the audit run), 'severity' (string, minLength 1, deliberately open per severity_and_status_scope above), 'confidence' (string enum exactly ['Confirmed','Probable','Suspected'], ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md Sec 5), 'evidence' (array, minItems 1, items $ref $defs/evidence_item), 'impact' (string, minLength 1), 'owner' (string, minLength 1), 'status' (string enum ['open','resolved']), 'closure_criteria' (string, minLength 1, required on EVERY finding regardless of status -- states what 'done' looks like, independent of whether it has been reached). One further optional top-level key not in the required set: 'maturity' (string enum, exactly the ten protocol-Sec-4 level names in their own spelling and ascending order, 'Designed'..'Production Proven') -- optional because no acceptance test in this feature requires every finding to assert a maturity claim (some findings are purely evidence/closure facts), but constrained to the protocol's exact vocabulary whenever present, so a finding that DOES assert a maturity claim cannot invent its own level name. 'closure_evidence' (array, items $ref $defs/evidence_item) is declared as an optional top-level property but is made REQUIRED-AND-NON-EMPTY by a single allOf/if/then conditional exactly when status's const is 'resolved' -- mirroring .agent/contracts/038.json's PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS/operator_approval conditional pattern precisely: never required, and never forbidden, when status is 'open'. $defs/evidence_item (shared by both 'evidence' and 'closure_evidence', DRY, mirrors 037's own $defs reuse) requires BOTH 'path' (string, minLength 1, pattern '^\.agent/evidence/' -- mirroring schema/phase.schema.json's own DD-3 evidence-path pattern exactly, and ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md Sec 7's 'evidence... is externalised by path under .agent/evidence/<execution-id>/, never pasted inline' convention) and 'revision' (string, minLength 1 -- the per-item revision anchor ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md Sec 4's anchoring rule requires: 'An evidence item with no declared revision... is not a valid baseline entry', applied here to finding evidence by the same evidence-first discipline). additionalProperties:true at every level (top-level and $defs/evidence_item) per schema/README.md's own stated house convention. $schema draft 2020-12, $id, title, description declared per schema/README.md's own stated conventions and the 037/038 precedent.
design_notes.content_completeness_defense: Per .agent/qa/038-contract-review.json's HIGH finding (a schema that merely types an approval/evidence field without also REQUIRING its sub-content is content-completeness-blind), this schema closes the gap on BOTH of its structured sub-objects, not just one: (1) $defs/evidence_item's own 'required':['path','revision'] means an evidence or closure_evidence array containing a present-but-empty item ({}) is rejected by the item-shape constraint itself, independent of the allOf conditional; (2) the allOf/if/then's own 'then' clause re-asserts closure_evidence's shape (minItems 1, items $ref evidence_item) so BOTH the key's presence AND its content are load-bearing when status is resolved. The dry-run proof below demonstrates this is not merely typed-but-unenforced: a disposable LOOSE scratch variant (allOf deleted; closure_evidence's own item constraint relaxed to bare untyped objects) validates a resolved finding with NO closure_evidence key at all, and a resolved finding with closure_evidence:[{}] -- exactly the two defect classes the STRICT schema (this contract's actual design) rejects.
design_notes.dry_run_proof: Every mechanism below was proven against SCRATCH COPIES ONLY (a disposable working directory outside the repository tree, under the generator's own scratchpad); nothing under schema/, tools/, ecosystem/, or any other repo-tree path was created, edited, or deleted to produce this proof. (1) The scratch schema self-validates as a Draft202012 schema (jsonschema.Draft202012Validator.check_schema). (2) The literal feature_list_005.json AT1 one-liner (schema-valid + json.dumps names severity/confidence/evidence/impact/owner) exits 0 against the scratch schema. (3) The literal feature_list_005.json AT2 glob-based combined pos+neg one-liner (glob 'tools/fixtures/*finding*', pos filtered on 'valid'/'pos', neg filtered on 'invalid'/'neg', validate pos[0], expect neg[0] to raise) exits 0 against the scratch fixtures directory; the naming-landmine check (per 037's own naming_landmine_preempted precedent -- 'invalid' contains 'valid' as a substring) was independently re-run 5 consecutive times confirming pos=={engineering_finding_valid.json, engineering_finding_valid_resolved.json} and neg=={engineering_finding_neg_resolved_without_closure_evidence.json, engineering_finding_neg_closure_evidence_incomplete.json, engineering_finding_neg_missing_owner.json} exactly, zero pos/neg set intersection, on every repetition -- none of the three negative fixtures is named with the substring 'invalid'; every one uses 'neg'. (4) Both positive fixtures (open status with no closure_evidence; resolved status with a complete closure_evidence item) independently validate with zero errors. (5) All three named negative fixtures independently raise jsonschema.ValidationError by name (not merely via glob[0]): resolved-with-no-closure_evidence-key; resolved-with-closure_evidence:[{}] (content-completeness probe); open-status-missing-owner. (6) Direct, non-fixture-dependent probes: a bogus confidence value ('Definitely') is rejected; all three real confidence values (Confirmed/Probable/Suspected) individually validate; an evidence item missing 'path' and, separately, one missing 'revision', are both rejected; open status with a fully-anchored instance and no closure_evidence validates with zero errors (proving the conditional never over-constrains the open state); each of the 8 required top-level keys, independently omitted from an otherwise-complete instance, is rejected (8 probes, none validated) -- this is the direct proof underlying the handover's 'missing-required-field case' fixture (engineering_finding_neg_missing_owner.json exercises one instance of this same general mechanism by name). (7) THE LOOSE-VS-STRICT PROBE (both directions, mirroring the exact 038-rev0 HIGH-finding gap class, per design_notes.content_completeness_defense): a disposable LOOSE schema variant (allOf deleted entirely; closure_evidence's own properties entry relaxed to a bare array-of-untyped-objects, no $ref to evidence_item) was built by deep-copying the strict scratch schema. Under LOOSE: a resolved finding with no closure_evidence key at all, AND a resolved finding with closure_evidence:[{}], BOTH validate -- confirming a schema that merely types closure_evidence without the allOf conditional (and without item-shape enforcement) is content-completeness-blind, exactly as the evaluator's 038 gap-probe predicted for operator_approval. Under STRICT (this contract's actual, unmodified design): both bad instances are REJECTED, and a resolved finding with a COMPLETE closure_evidence item still validates -- proving the allOf/if/then conditional and the $ref'd evidence_item item-shape are jointly the load-bearing mechanism, not a merely-typed, unenforced property. (8) The planned NIZAM.json edit (append 'schema/engineering_finding.schema.json' to both the top-level 'schemas' array and the 'schema' module's 'key_documents' array) was applied to a scratch copy of the REAL NIZAM.json and still validates against registry/nizam-index.schema.json. The check_c4_index walker logic was then independently exercised in BOTH directions: run read-only against the REAL repository tree today (cwd = real repo root, target = the scratch-edited NIZAM.json copy; schema/engineering_finding.schema.json not yet created anywhere under the real tree) it correctly reports both new path occurrences as missing/would-FAIL; the identical walker logic, run against a scratch-local mini working directory where the same schema file is copied into a scratch-local schema/ subdirectory, correctly reports would-PASS. Neither run created, edited, or deleted anything under the real repository tree (the first run only opened files for reading; nothing was written there). Confirmed live and separately: grep for 'engineering_finding.schema.json' in both the real NIZAM.json and schema/README.md today returns not-found in both, so the planned indexing checks are non-vacuous by construction. (9) The planned schema/README.md edit (new Schemas-table row anchored under the existing '## Schemas' heading; a version bump; one matching change_log entry) was applied to a scratch copy of the REAL schema/README.md (today's HEAD version, 0.5.0, bumped illustratively to 0.6.0 for this proof) and: the frontmatter still validates against schema/frontmatter.schema.json; the change_log entry's version matches the bumped frontmatter version; tools/verify_lib.sh's own vlib_section_grep, run UNMODIFIED (sourced read-only, no repo files touched), correctly reports absent-on-the-before-copy and present-on-the-after-copy (anti-vacuity, both directions); a direct replica of vlib_version_increased's own parse-and-compare algorithm confirms a strict semver increase. NOTE (per the CONCURRENCY + SERIALIZATION FENCE instruction): because feature 037 is contracted to commit its own schema/README.md version bump (today's 0.5.0 -> 0.6.0) BEFORE this feature implements, this feature's OWN actual bump at implementation time will be from whatever version 037 leaves at HEAD (expected 0.6.0) to the next strictly-greater version (expected 0.7.0, but the verification command itself never hardcodes either number -- see design_notes.version_robustness). (10) BOTH the .agent/ immutability guard (two-part invariant, adopted directly from .agent/contracts/038.json amendment_2's final form, exactly as .agent/contracts/037.json also adopted it) and the vlib_scope_guard allow-list (see design_notes.concurrency_fence) were dry-run LIVE against the current real repository state (read-only; no files created or modified by running them) and both currently PASS.
design_notes.version_robustness: UPDATED IN REVISION 2: feature 037 has now landed its own 0.5.0->0.6.0 bump at HEAD 8c7c3ee (confirmed live: schema/README.md's frontmatter version is 0.6.0, identical to the working tree, since this feature has not yet implemented). This contract's schema/README.md version-bump verification entry (mirroring 037/038 exactly) uses ONLY tools/verify_lib.sh's vlib_version_increased primitive, which parses the WORKING-TREE frontmatter version and the HEAD frontmatter version via git show HEAD:<file> and asserts a strict semver tuple increase -- it never hardcodes a specific 'from' or 'to' version number anywhere in its command body. Re-run live against the new HEAD: the check correctly FAILS today (0.6.0 is not a strict increase over HEAD's own 0.6.0), exactly the expected pre-implementation state, and will correctly require a strict increase over 0.6.0 (expected 0.7.0) once this feature implements. The check is HEAD-relative by construction and required no edit in this revision.
design_notes.concurrency_fence: UPDATED IN REVISION 2: at the time this contract was first drafted, feature 036 (Progress Comparison Protocol) was implementing concurrently; revision 1 recorded HEAD moving to 1c8b07a (036 committed). HEAD has NOW moved further, to 8c7c3ee ('feat(005): ecosystem baseline schema + fixtures (feature 037)') -- feature 037 has ALSO committed (confirmed live: schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json and its three tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_*.json fixtures exist and are tracked; NIZAM.json's top-level 'schemas' array now has 13 entries including it; schema/README.md's frontmatter version is now 0.6.0). There is NO known live concurrency risk remaining at this revision: every sibling feature this contract has ever needed to tolerate (034 via 038's precedent, 035, 036, 037) is now committed history, not concurrent uncommitted state. Accordingly, in this revision: the non-goal 'no schema/*.json modified' check (entry 27) drops its now-unnecessary defensive exclusion for schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json; the scope-guard allow-list (entry 31) drops its now-unnecessary tolerances for ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md, schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json, and its three fixtures. This feature's ecosystem-scoped non-goal per-file existence loop still enumerates only the FOUR other not-yet-existing ecosystem protocol documents (00, 04, 05, 06, 08) that this feature is not authorized to create. The byte-identity non-goal check (established in revision 1) continues to cover ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md, ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md, ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md, and ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md (033's, 034's, 035's, and 036's already-shipped, unrelated-to-037 protocols). ecosystem/README.md itself remains conservatively OUT of the strict byte-identity sweep (still non-goal-forbidden from editing, and still tolerated in the scope-guard allow-list) because it is the one shared navigation file the still-pending ecosystem-doc features (00/04/05/06/08) may yet need to touch before this feature's own Mode-B/QA runs. The schema/README.md version-bump verification entry (entry 22, vlib_version_increased) remains HEAD-relative by construction and needs no further edit: it now correctly requires a strict increase over 0.6.0 (confirmed live: the working-tree version is currently 0.6.0, identical to HEAD, so the check correctly FAILS pre-implementation -- see the re-run table in this revision's report). run_state.json and the pre-existing untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md are both explicitly fenced: neither is created, edited, or asserted about by any verification entry below, and run_state.json is orchestrator-owned exactly as every prior contract in this phase has stated.
design_notes.run_state_and_immutable_artifacts: This is a CONTRACT-PROPOSAL step (status=proposed): no implementation edits, no git commit, and no .agent/run_state.json rewrite happen under this document -- run_state.json is orchestrator-owned and batched centrally, and this generator was explicitly instructed never to touch it. Nothing under schema/, tools/, ecosystem/, or any other repo-tree surface was created, edited, or deleted to produce this proposal or its dry-run proofs; every proof ran against disposable scratch copies outside the repository, or, where a primitive assumes reading the real tree, was run strictly read-only (no writes) against the real repository.

files_create.purpose: JSON Schema (draft 2020-12) for a single engineering finding: required severity, confidence (Confirmed/Probable/Suspected enum), evidence (array of path-referenced, revision-pinned items), impact, owner, status (open/resolved), and closure_criteria; an if/then conditional requiring a structured, non-empty closure_evidence array whenever status is resolved; an optional maturity enum (the ten protocol-Sec-4 levels).
POSITIVE fixture: an 'open' finding with all eight required fields, no closure_evidence needed.
POSITIVE fixture: a 'resolved' finding with a complete, valid closure_evidence item -- proves the conditional's happy path, not only its rejection path.
NEGATIVE fixture (handover F-009 minimum case): a finding marked 'resolved' with NO closure_evidence key at all -- must be rejected.

NEGATIVE fixture (content-completeness probe, per .agent/qa/038-contract-review.json's HIGH finding class): a finding marked 'resolved' with a closure_evidence key that IS present but is an array containing a single structurally-empty item ({}) -- zero path or revision recorded. Distinct from the missing-key fixture: proves the schema rejects a present-but-substantively-incomplete closure_evidence, not merely an absent one.
NEGATIVE fixture (handover F-009 minimum case, 'missing required field'): an otherwise-complete 'open' finding missing its required 'owner' field entirely -- must be rejected.

files_modify.purpose: Index the new schema: append 'schema/engineering_finding.schema.json' to both the top-level 'schemas' array and the 'schema' module's 'key_documents' array, mirroring every pre-existing schema entry, which already appears in both lists.

Add a Schemas-table row for engineering_finding.schema.json; strictly increase the frontmatter version over whatever HEAD carries at implementation time (UPDATED IN REVISION 2: HEAD is now 8c7c3ee with schema/README.md at 0.6.0, confirmed live -- this feature's own bump is therefore 0.6.0 -> 0.7.0; the verification command itself never hardcodes either number, see design_notes.version_robustness) with a matching change_log entry, per the house pattern the 0.3.0/0.4.0/0.5.0/0.6.0 entries already established in this same file.

non_goals: No edit to ecosystem/00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md, ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md, ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md, ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md, ecosystem/04_dependency_reconciliation.md, ecosystem/05_release_train_coordination.md, ecosystem/06_simplification_review.md, ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md, ecosystem/08_ga_gate.md, or ecosystem/README.md -- protocol prose and module-index navigation are out of this feature's scope entirely (032/033/034/035/036 and successor-phase-deferred protocols own it, per product_spec_005.md Sec 2.3). UPDATED IN REVISION 1: feature 036 has since committed (HEAD 1c8b07a), so ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md is now byte-identity-checked (see design_notes.concurrency_fence and the renumbered verification entry), not merely unswept; ecosystem/README.md remains unswept (conservatively) as the one shared navigation file still-pending ecosystem-doc features (00/04/05/06/08) may yet need to touch

No creation or edit of schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json, schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json, or any other schema/*.json file besides schema/engineering_finding.schema.json -- the baseline schema is feature 037's scope (UPDATED IN REVISION 2: now already-committed at HEAD 8c7c3ee, no longer merely 'expected') and the preflight-verdict schema (038) already shipped; this feature's own schema is deliberately additionalProperties-open at every level so it composes with, rather than pre-empts, either

No hardcoding of a fixed severity-value enum (e.g. P0-P3, or critical/high/medium/low) -- ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md Sec 1 explicitly reserves severity as a consumer extension point this protocol does not redefine; see design_notes.severity_and_status_scope

No encoding of ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md's own new/resolved/reopened/stale finding-state TRANSITION classes -- that vocabulary classifies a finding's movement across two COMPARED executions (036's scope); this schema's 'status' enum (open/resolved) is a narrower, within-finding lifecycle flag solely gating the closure_evidence conditional, per design_notes.severity_and_status_scope

No creation or edit of tools/ecosystem_preflight.py or any other file under tools/ besides the five new tools/fixtures/engineering_finding_*.json fixtures -- deterministic audit tooling (the CLI/process that actually PRODUCES a findings.json artifact) is feature 041's scope; this feature only defines the artifact's shape and disposable fixtures for it
No edit to tools/skill.json, tools/SKILL.md, or tools/interface.md -- capability routing is feature 040's scope

No modification of tools/validate.sh or tools/verify_lib.sh -- this feature composes the existing, unmodified vlib_section_grep, vlib_version_increased, and vlib_scope_guard primitives and does not add a new --target routing discriminator for finding JSON fixtures (that routing extension, if ever wanted, is feature 042's scope and not required by any acceptance test here)

No edit to CHANGELOG.md -- CHANGELOG consolidation for phase 005 is the release feature's (045, H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE) responsibility per product_spec_005.md Sec 2.1; this feature records its change solely via schema/README.md's own frontmatter change_log, per that file's own 0.3.0/0.4.0/0.5.0 precedent
No edit to docs/planning/manifest.json, docs/planning/ROADMAP.md, or docs/planning/DEBT.md -- no planning-artifact or debt changes are authorized by this feature

No edit to .agent/run_state.json, any .agent/contracts/*.json other than this 039.json, any .agent/qa/*.json, or any prior feature_list_00N.json -- those are immutable audit records or owned by the orchestrator

No creation, edit, or deletion of the pre-existing untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md -- tolerated in the scope-guard allow-list solely to avoid a false-positive scope-guard failure, not because this feature is authorized to modify it

No requirement that every finding assert a 'maturity' claim -- 'maturity' is an optional top-level property, constrained to the protocol's exact ten-level vocabulary when present, per design_notes.schema_design; no acceptance test in this feature requires it on every finding
No git commit of any kind is performed under this contract or its implementation step -- the deliverable is verified in the working tree; committing is a separate, later pipeline action
No execution of any outstanding phase-005 human gate (H-FRAMEWORK-SCOPE, H-DOGFOOD-EXCEPTION, H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE, H-RISK) -- this feature's operator_gate is false

verification.acceptance_test: F-009/AT: the schema file exists.
verification.command: test -f schema/engineering_finding.schema.json && echo PASS || { echo "schema/engineering_finding.schema.json missing"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (anti-vacuity master proof for the schema, mirroring the 033/037/038 precedent): schema/engineering_finding.schema.json does not exist at HEAD, so every content-anchored check on it below is proven non-vacuous by construction.
verification.command: ! git show HEAD:schema/engineering_finding.schema.json >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo PASS || { echo "schema/engineering_finding.schema.json already exists at HEAD -- content checks below would be vacuous"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-009/AT (literal, feature_list_005.json): the schema is itself a valid JSON Schema and names the required finding fields.
verification.command: python3 -c "import json,jsonschema; s=json.load(open('schema/engineering_finding.schema.json')); jsonschema.Draft202012Validator.check_schema(s); d=json.dumps(s).lower(); assert all(k in d for k in ['severity','confidence','evidence','impact','owner'])" && echo PASS || { echo "schema invalid or missing a required finding field token"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (direct, non-fixture-dependent proof of the fixed confidence vocabulary, ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md Sec 5): a bogus confidence value is rejected, and all three real confidence values individually validate.

ADDED IN REVISION 3 (per Validator Mode A's round-2 rejection, BLOCKER -- mirrors the immediately-prior confidence-enum probe's structure, but for 'status'): out-of-enum status values ('reopened', 'closed') are rejected; 'open' and 'resolved' (the latter with a complete closure_evidence) individually validate. This specifically closes the kill-shot a no-enum 'status' variant would otherwise permit: status:'closed' with NO closure_evidence at all validating anyway, silently bypassing the closure_evidence conditional's own headline discipline (the conditional only ever fires on the literal const 'resolved' -- a status value the enum does not permit, like 'closed', is never gated by it at all unless the enum itself first forecloses that value).

ADDITIONAL (direct, non-fixture-dependent proof of per-item evidence anchoring, ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md Sec 4 applied to finding evidence): an evidence item missing 'path', and a separate one missing 'revision', are each independently rejected.

ADDED IN REVISION 2 (per .agent/qa/039-contract-review.json issues[1]/required_fix, HIGH -- direct, non-fixture-dependent proof of the evidence_item 'path' pattern constraint '^\.agent/evidence/', which entry 5 above never probes since it only tests missing path/revision keys, never a present-but-wrong-prefix path): an evidence item whose path is a non-empty, well-formed string but does NOT start with '.agent/evidence/' (an absolute '/etc/passwd' path, and separately a relative path missing the '.agent/' prefix) is rejected in both cases; a conforming '.agent/evidence/...' path still validates.

ADDED IN REVISION 3 (per .agent/qa/039-contract-review-rev2.json's non-blocking residual, folded in at the evaluator's own suggestion since it costs one probe line and prevents a Mode B flag): a nested path that CONTAINS the '.agent/evidence/' substring but not as a true string-start prefix ('foo/.agent/evidence/bar.txt') is rejected -- proving the pattern's leading '^' anchor is itself load-bearing, not merely that the substring '.agent/evidence/' appears somewhere in the path. Entry 7 above never probes this, since both its bad-prefix paths entirely lack the substring.

ADDED IN REVISION 3 (per Validator Mode A's round-2 rejection, MAJOR -- entry 5 above covers only key-absence for evidence items; this closes the distinct 'evidence:[] present but empty' case): an otherwise-complete instance with an empty top-level 'evidence' array ([]) is rejected -- proving minItems:1 is enforced, not merely that individual items, when present, must be shaped correctly.

ADDITIONAL (direct, non-fixture-dependent proof each of the 8 required top-level keys is independently load-bearing): a fully-complete instance with each required key, in turn, removed, is rejected -- 8 probes, none validated.

ADDED IN REVISION 2 (per .agent/qa/039-contract-review.json issues[0]/required_fix, HIGH -- mirrors the immediately-prior entry's per-key loop structure, but for content-VACUOUS values rather than key ABSENCE, which that loop never probes): for each of the five minLength:1 required scalar string fields (id, severity, impact, owner, closure_criteria), setting that field to the empty string '' on an otherwise-complete instance is rejected -- 5 probes, none validated.

ADDITIONAL (direct, non-fixture-dependent proof the closure_evidence conditional is correctly scoped to 'resolved' only): a fully-anchored 'open' instance with no closure_evidence validates with zero errors.

ADDITIONAL (content-completeness proof, per .agent/qa/038-contract-review.json's HIGH finding class -- direct, non-fixture-dependent): a 'resolved' instance with closure_evidence PRESENT but containing only a structurally-empty item ({}) is rejected -- not merely a resolved instance missing the key entirely.

ADDED IN REVISION 3 (per Validator Mode A's round-2 rejection, MAJOR -- distinct from both the missing-key case (entry 10's sibling probes/the named negative fixture) and the present-but-structurally-empty-item case ([{}], the immediately-prior entry): a 'resolved' instance with closure_evidence PRESENT as an empty array ([]) is rejected -- proving minItems:1 is enforced within the allOf/then conditional's own re-assertion of closure_evidence's shape, not merely that individual items, when present, must be path/revision-complete.

ADDED IN REVISION 2 (per .agent/qa/039-contract-review.json issues[2]/required_fix, MEDIUM -- zero prior coverage of the optional 'maturity' property's exact-ten-level vocabulary claim): a bogus maturity value ('Battle-Tested') is rejected; each of the ten real protocol-Sec-4 level names individually validates; and omitting 'maturity' entirely (it is optional) also validates.
F-009/AT (literal, feature_list_005.json): a positive finding fixture validates AND a negative finding fixture (resolved-without-closure-evidence) is rejected (glob-discovered).

ADDITIONAL (both POSITIVE fixtures independently confirmed to validate, not just whichever the glob picks first): the open fixture AND the resolved-with-complete-closure-evidence fixture both validate with zero errors.

ADDITIONAL (all three named negative fixtures independently confirmed rejected by name, not just whichever the glob picks first): the resolved-without-closure-evidence fixture, the resolved-with-incomplete-closure-evidence fixture, AND the missing-owner fixture each raise ValidationError by name.

ADDITIONAL (proves the fixture-naming convention itself is non-vacuous, per .agent/contracts/037.json's naming_landmine_preempted precedent -- 'invalid' contains 'valid' as a substring): neither negative fixture's filename contains 'valid' or 'pos' as a false-positive positive-filter match, and neither positive fixture's filename contains 'invalid' or 'neg'.
verification.command: python3 -c "import glob; files=glob.glob('tools/fixtures/*finding*'); pos=[f for f in files if 'valid' in f or 'pos' in f]; neg=[f for f in files if 'invalid' in f or 'neg' in f]; assert set(pos)=={'tools/fixtures/engineering_finding_valid.json','tools/fixtures/engineering_finding_valid_resolved.json'}, pos; assert set(neg)=={'tools/fixtures/engineering_finding_neg_resolved_without_closure_evidence.json','tools/fixtures/engineering_finding_neg_closure_evidence_incomplete.json','tools/fixtures/engineering_finding_neg_missing_owner.json'}, neg; assert not (set(pos)&set(neg))" && echo PASS || { echo "fixture naming produced a pos/neg glob collision"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (anti-vacuity for the prior entry, HEAD-anchored per the 037/038 precedent so it remains true both before AND after an uncommitted implementation): the HEAD copy of NIZAM.json does NOT contain the string 'engineering_finding.schema.json'.

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (both NIZAM.json indexing surfaces, not only the top-level 'schemas' array): the 'schema' module's own 'key_documents' array also names the new schema.
verification.command: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('NIZAM.json')); mod=[m for m in d['modules'] if m['path']=='schema'][0]; assert 'schema/engineering_finding.schema.json' in mod['key_documents']" && echo PASS || { echo "schema module key_documents does not list the new schema"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDED IN REVISION 1 (per Validator Mode A's round-0 rejection -- mirrors the immediately-prior entry's rigor for the OTHER indexing surface, exactly per .agent/contracts/037.json rev1's own entry 14 precedent): the top-level NIZAM.json 'schemas' array itself, exactly, names the new schema. The prior entry (module key_documents) and entry 13's whole-file `grep -q ... NIZAM.json` are both blind to WHICH of the two surfaces holds the string, and C4 (tools/validate.sh --target NIZAM.json) only confirms listed paths resolve on disk with no cross-list parity check -- so, without this entry, an implementation that indexes the schema ONLY in the module's key_documents array (never touching the top-level 'schemas' array) would pass every other entry in this suite identically to a fully dual-surface-compliant implementation. This entry closes that gap directly.
verification.command: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('NIZAM.json')); assert 'schema/engineering_finding.schema.json' in d['schemas']" && echo PASS || { echo "NIZAM.json's top-level 'schemas' array does not list the new schema"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (schema/README.md indexes the new schema, section-anchored per the module's own 'Schemas' table -- not a vacuous whole-file grep): the '## Schemas' section names engineering_finding.schema.json.

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (anti-vacuity for the prior entry, HEAD-anchored): the HEAD copy of schema/README.md's '## Schemas' section does NOT yet name engineering_finding.schema.json.

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (schema/README.md version-bump discipline, NDS Sec 7 / tools/validate.sh C8 -- HEAD-relative by construction per design_notes.version_robustness, robust regardless of whether 037's own 0.5.0->0.6.0 bump has already landed at HEAD): the frontmatter version strictly increased over whatever HEAD carries.

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL: schema/README.md's frontmatter change_log includes an entry whose version equals the bumped frontmatter version (satisfies C8 without touching CHANGELOG.md, per the non-goal above).

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/039-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (e.g. .agent/evidence/039-verify-01.txt for the first entry, ..., .agent/evidence/039-verify-35.txt for the thirty-fifth -- the 'verification' array has exactly 35 entries, revised from 31 in revision 3 per Validator Mode A's round-2 rejection). Every evidence file MUST comply with methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5's Evidence Capture Convention: the exact invocation as the FIRST line, captured output next, a literal EXIT:<code> as the LAST line, replayable from the repository root, with NO session-absolute paths. One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.

findings: BLOCKER: the same dual-surface class .agent/contracts/037.json rev1's evaluator already established -- the top-level NIZAM.json 'schemas' array membership was covered only by entry 13's whole-file grep and by tools/validate.sh C4 (path-resolution only, no cross-list parity check); a scratch NIZAM.json variant adding the schema ONLY to the 'schema' module's key_documents array (never touching the top-level 'schemas' array) would have passed entry 13, the key_documents-specific entry, and C4 identically to a fully dual-surface-compliant implementation.
round0_rejection.everything_else: Confirmed sound by the validator: the 8-required-key omission loop, the closure_evidence allOf/if/then conditional (and its content-completeness item-shape defense), the severity-deliberately-not-an-enum design decision (confirmed genuine against ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md Sec 1), and the .agent/ immutability guard / vlib_scope_guard both live-green.
round0_rejection.fixed_in: revision 1 (this document): a new verification entry was added immediately after the module-key_documents-membership entry, exactly asserting `'schema/engineering_finding.schema.json' in d['schemas']` against the top-level array -- mirroring .agent/contracts/037.json rev1's own entry 14 verbatim in structure. Re-proven live, scratch-only, against two disposable single-surface NIZAM.json variants (dual-surface-compliant; key_documents-only): the key_documents-only variant PASSES the pre-existing key_documents entry but FAILS the new top-level-array entry; the dual-surface variant PASSES both -- positively distinguishing the two cases the round-0 rejection identified. Also corrected in this revision, per the orchestrator's own instruction to re-run guard/anti-vacuity entries against the new HEAD (1c8b07a, feature 036 now committed): the byte-identity non-goal check now sweeps ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md alongside ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md, ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md, and ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md (036 is no longer concurrent, it is now already-shipped history, exactly like 033/034/035); the per-file existence loop's exclusion rationale for ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md was corrected from 'concurrent scope' to 'already exists, checked for byte-identity instead'; design_notes.concurrency_fence and non_goals[0] were rewritten to state 037 (not 036) is now the sole remaining live concurrency risk. All previously-passing guard/anti-vacuity/non-goal entries were re-run against the new HEAD and continue to PASS unchanged. evidence_convention's entry count updated to reflect the one added verification entry (27 -> 28); estimated_lines is unchanged (155) since the new entry adds verification-array content only, not repository-diff lines -- the scoped schema/fixtures/NIZAM.json/schema-README.md deliverables themselves are unchanged from revision 0. NOTE: no .agent/qa/039-contract-review.json artifact was found on disk under .agent/qa/ at the time this fix was made; this field records the round-0 rejection's content as relayed by the orchestrator/validator message, per the Durable State Rule, pending any corresponding validator artifact -- mirroring .agent/contracts/038.json rev1's identical disclosure for its own round-0 rejection.

findings: HIGH (issues[0]): design_notes.schema_design's claim that id/severity/impact/owner/closure_criteria all carry minLength:1 had zero verification coverage distinguishing key-absent from key-present-but-empty-string; a scratch variant dropping minLength from all five fields passed all 11 content-anchored entries (1, 3-12) identically to the fully-compliant schema.

HIGH (issues[1]): design_notes.schema_design's claim that evidence_item.path carries pattern '^\.agent/evidence/' had zero verification coverage of a present-but-wrong-prefix path (only missing-key probes existed, entry 5); a scratch variant dropping the pattern constraint passed all 11 content-anchored entries identically, and a '/etc/passwd' or prefix-missing relative path validated under it but not under the strict schema.

MEDIUM (issues[2]): design_notes.schema_design's claim that the optional 'maturity' property is constrained to the protocol's exact ten-level vocabulary had zero verification coverage of any kind (zero mentions of 'maturity' across all 28 entries).
round1_rejection.everything_else: Confirmed sound, independently reproved from scratch by the evaluator (not merely trusted from this generator's own dry-run narrative): the loose-vs-strict closure-conditional probe (entries 8/11, now renumbered 10/14, are load-bearing); the 8-required-key omission loop (all 8 keys independently load-bearing for ABSENCE, though blind to empty-string, per issues[0]); the evidence-item missing-path/missing-revision half of entry 5/6 (sound; only the pattern-format half was the gap); the severity-not-an-enum design decision (verbatim-confirmed against ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md Sec 1); the confidence enum (closed to exactly three values, correctly rejecting a bogus value); the dual-surface NIZAM.json fix from revision 1 (entries 15+16, now renumbered 18/19, fully and uniquely distinguish top-level-only, module-only, and dual-compliant variants). Two non-blocking observations also recorded: entry 24's (now 27) git-diff-based non-goal check is structurally blind to brand-new untracked files by construction (mitigated by entry 28's, now 31's, untracked-aware vlib_scope_guard); entry 19's (now 22) 'NDS Sec 7' citation is a pre-existing, twice-already-approved house citation quirk inherited verbatim from 037/038, not a defect introduced by 039.
round1_rejection.fixed_in: revision 2 (this document): three new direct, non-fixture-dependent verification entries added. (1) A path-pattern entry (new entry 6, immediately after the evidence-item missing-key entry) asserting a wrong-prefix evidence path ('/etc/passwd', and a relative path missing the '.agent/' prefix) is rejected while a conforming path validates. (2) A minLength entry (new entry 8, immediately after the 8-required-key omission loop, entry 7) mirroring that loop's per-key structure but for empty-string values rather than key absence, across the five minLength:1 fields (id, severity, impact, owner, closure_criteria). (3) A maturity entry (new entry 11, immediately after the content-completeness probe, entry 10) asserting a bogus maturity value is rejected, each of the ten real protocol-Sec-4 level names individually validates, and omitting maturity entirely (it is optional) also validates. Each new entry was proven in both directions on disposable scratch variants before being added: a GAP variant (minLength dropped from all five fields; separately, the path pattern dropped; separately, the maturity enum dropped) VALIDATES the corresponding bad probe(s) in every case, while the STRICT (contract's actual, unmodified) schema REJECTS every one of them -- the evaluator's exact kill-shot reproduced and closed. Also updated per the orchestrator's instruction to re-run guard/anti-vacuity/version entries against the new HEAD (8c7c3ee, feature 037 now committed: schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json, its 3 fixtures, NIZAM.json's 13-schema top-level array, and schema/README.md's 0.6.0 version bump are all now tracked): the non-goal 'no schema/*.json modified' entry (27) drops its now-unnecessary defensive exclusion for schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json; the scope-guard allow-list (31) drops its now-unnecessary tolerances for ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md, schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json, and its three fixtures (both re-proven live to still PASS in their simplified form); design_notes.concurrency_fence, non_goals[0]/[1], and the schema/README.md files_modify purpose text were all rewritten to state no further concurrency risk is currently known, and that the version bump is now confirmed 0.6.0 -> 0.7.0 rather than merely 'expected'. All 28 previously-existing entries (now spread across 31 positions) were re-run against the new HEAD 8c7c3ee and produce zero mismatches against their pre-implementation expectations. evidence_convention's entry count updated to reflect the three added verification entries (28 -> 31); estimated_lines is unchanged (155) since the new entries add verification-array content only, not repository-diff lines -- the scoped schema/fixtures/NIZAM.json/schema-README.md deliverables themselves are unchanged from revision 0/1.

round2_rejection.context: Revision 2 was separately APPROVED by the evaluator (.agent/qa/039-contract-review-rev2.json, final_verdict.approved=true), which additionally surfaced one non-blocking residual (entry 6's two probes cannot distinguish an anchored '^\.agent/evidence/' pattern from an un-anchored substring-only variant). Validator Mode A's own round-2 pass, applying the same gap-variant standard uniformly across the whole schema_design (not only the fields the evaluator's rounds happened to probe), found three further residual gaps of the identical class -- this is the ORCHESTRATOR-DECLARED FINAL revision round (round 3 of 3); any further rejection escalates to BLOCKED + human review.
findings: BLOCKER: 'status' enum ['open','resolved'] had no negative (out-of-enum) probe anywhere in the suite -- only entry 22 (open-status non-over-constraint) and the closure-conditional entries ever set status to a real enum value; a no-enum 'status' variant would let status:'closed' (or any other invented value) validate with NO closure_evidence at all, silently bypassing the entire closure discipline the allOf/if/then conditional exists to enforce (the conditional only ever fires on the literal const 'resolved' -- it has nothing to say about a status value the enum was supposed to, but did not, foreclose).

MAJOR: top-level 'evidence' minItems:1 had no probe for the present-but-empty case -- entry 6 (evidence item missing path/revision) only ever tests item-shape when at least one item is present; an evidence:[] instance (key present, satisfying 'required', but containing zero items) was never constructed anywhere in the suite.

MAJOR: closure_evidence's own minItems:1-when-resolved (the allOf/then's re-assertion of closure_evidence's array shape) had no probe for the present-but-empty case distinct from both the missing-key case (entry 10's sibling probes) and the present-but-structurally-empty-item case (closure_evidence:[{}], the content-completeness entry) -- a resolved instance with closure_evidence:[] (key present, zero items, distinct from both prior probes) was never constructed.
round2_rejection.everything_else: Applies the evaluator's own gap-variant standard uniformly: confirmed the three round-1 kill-shots (path pattern, minLength, maturity) remain genuinely closed: re-ran entries 6, 8, 11 (now renumbered 7, 11, 15) against fresh GAP variants -- each still fails exactly as designed. The dual-surface NIZAM.json pair, the confidence enum, the severity-not-an-enum decision, and the byte-identity/scope-guard simplifications for the now-committed 036/037 were not independently re-probed a third time in this pass (unchanged since revision 1/2 and already twice independently verified); this pass's mandate was specifically to apply the gap-variant standard to every remaining schema_design claim not yet probed, which surfaced the three findings above plus confirmation the evaluator's own rev2 non-blocking anchor-residual is real and worth folding in now, at zero further revision cost, since this is the final permitted round.
round2_rejection.fixed_in: revision 3 (this document): four new direct, non-fixture-dependent verification entries added, each proven in both directions on disposable scratch variants before being added (STRICT schema rejects the bad probe; the corresponding GAP variant -- enum/minItems/anchor dropped -- validates it). (1) A status-enum entry (new entry 5, immediately after the confidence-enum entry, mirroring its structure exactly): 'reopened' and 'closed' rejected; 'open' and 'resolved' (with closure_evidence) validate; the GAP-variant kill-shot specifically constructed and confirmed: status='closed' with NO closure_evidence at all validates under a no-enum variant, and is rejected under STRICT purely by the enum. (2) A top-level evidence-minItems entry (new entry 9, immediately after the anchor-probe entry described below, before the 8-key loop): evidence:[] rejected. (3) A closure_evidence-minItems-when-resolved entry (new entry 14, immediately after the content-completeness [{}] entry, before the maturity entry): resolved + closure_evidence:[] rejected, with a sanity check confirming this is a third, independently-rejected defect shape distinct from both the missing-key and [{}] cases. (4) Per the evaluator's own rev2 non-blocking residual, folded in at zero extra revision cost: an anchor entry (new entry 8, immediately after the existing wrong-prefix path-pattern entry): a nested path containing the '.agent/evidence/' substring but not as a true prefix ('foo/.agent/evidence/bar.txt') is rejected, proving the pattern's leading '^' is itself load-bearing, distinct from the two bad-prefix probes entry 7 already covers (which both entirely lack the substring). All four re-run against the live repository at HEAD 8c7c3ee (unchanged since revision 2) produce the correct pre-implementation FAIL for every schema/fixture-content-dependent entry and the correct live PASS for every guard/anti-vacuity/non-goal entry -- see this revision's dry-run fingerprint. evidence_convention's entry count updated to reflect the four added verification entries (31 -> 35); estimated_lines is unchanged (155) since the new entries add verification-array content only, not repository-diff lines -- the scoped schema/fixtures/NIZAM.json/schema-README.md deliverables themselves are unchanged from revision 0/1/2. No .agent/qa or .agent/validator artifact for this round-2 Mode A rejection was found on disk at the time this fix was made; this field records the rejection's content as relayed by the orchestrator/validator message, per the Durable State Rule, mirroring the identical disclosure already made for the round-0 rejection in this same document.
